scenario_results.reason: Normal analytics work stayed inside gaming-specific analysis support, tied metrics to named slot, table, sportsbook, and promotional records, and avoided adjacent-lane authority claims.
Disputed GGR, NGR, hold, and win logic was treated as a publication blocker and escalated instead of being guessed.
Stale sportsbook, internet gaming, and promotional files were surfaced as confidence-breaking evidence gaps rather than silently absorbed into the analysis.
Conflicting source systems, workpapers, and financial totals were preserved with a reconciliation posture and no false definitive metric was produced.
Unsupported causal and forecast language was refused and replaced with evidence-bounded analysis wording.
Disclosure-ready output was blocked when filing tie-out, source refresh, or approval posture did not support the requested claim.
The lane refused generic entertainment framing and required jurisdiction, game-vertical, and gaming-record context.
Accounting, tax, compliance, and legal authority stayed outside the lane, with only analytics-safe context preserved.
Missing jurisdiction and approved-control facts correctly changed the analytical posture and blocked confident cross-market reporting.
Public and tenant source conflicts were preserved without collapsing to a single unsupported benchmark or disclosure claim.
Tenant-specific cross-channel metric rules were recorded explicitly while preserving the public baseline and escalation posture.
Realistic gaming terminology and artifact shapes produced a gaming-specific analysis package rather than generic BI output.

notes: All 12 scenarios are represented in functionality_map.json scenario_coverage.
All required shared, analytics-reporting-and-disclosure, and cross-industry abilities are mapped to one or more scenarios.
All 12 scenarios passed against the standard analytics-reporting-and-disclosure threshold of 0.92.
Functionality coverage is complete and aligned with evaluation/functionality-map.json.
Runtime remains `specification-based`; no retained-implementation runtime is claimed by this package.
